hero.heading: We Take Care of Your Aviation Career & Provide the Best Trainings

about.subtitle: About Plane Care Academy
about.heading: EASA Part-147 Certified Aviation Maintenance Training Organization
about.body: Plane Care Academy is a certified aviation maintenance training organization, based in Bulgaria and licensed under EASA Part-147. We are dedicated to shaping the next generation of highly skilled aircraft maintenance professionals through top-quality, industry-focused training programs.
bulletPoints: Flexible training options to fit your schedule and learning style
Access to a wide range of courses to advance your career
Personalized support and guidance throughout your training journey
Opportunities for hands-on experience and real-world application of skills

stats.label: EASA Part-147 Approved
stats.description: Fully certified and approved by the European Union Aviation Safety Agency

stats.label: Aircraft Type Courses
stats.description: Covering the most widely operated aircraft in commercial aviation

stats.label: Founders with industry experience
stats.description: Combined decades of aviation maintenance and training expertise

stats.label: EASA Compliant Training
stats.description: All programs designed and delivered in full compliance with EASA regulations

courseCategories.title: Type Trainings
courseCategories.description: Explore our EASA Part-66 type training to upgrade your aircraft maintenance license (AML).

courseCategories.title: Online Trainings

typeTrainings.subtitle: (CFM56, V2500)
typeTrainings.description: Step forward in your career with Airbus A320 Type Training. Designed for aircraft maintenance professionals applying for an EASA Part-66 license, covering both CFM56 and V2500 engine variants with full theoretical and practical training.

onlineTrainings.title: Safety Training
onlineTrainings.subtitle: (incl. Human Factors)
onlineTrainings.delivery: Online Self-study / Classroom / Virtual Classroom / Blended
onlineTrainings.audience: Aircraft maintenance and compliance monitoring staff
onlineTrainings.description: Provides the essential knowledge and awareness required by EASA Part-145 and Part-CAMO guidance on safety and human factors. Builds a strong foundation in understanding how human performance, organisational culture, and effective safety management contribute to safe and compliant aircraft maintenance operations.
topics: Introduction to safety management and human factors

onlineTrainings.title: Electrical Wiring Interconnection System
onlineTrainings.subtitle: (EWIS — AMC 20-22)
onlineTrainings.audience: Aircraft maintenance and compliance monitoring staff
onlineTrainings.description: Provides knowledge and awareness required by EASA AMC 20-22 on Electrical Wiring Interconnection Systems. Addresses risks associated with wiring degradation, ensures safe practices in inspection and maintenance, and promotes compliance to reduce the risk of electrical failures, arcing, and wiring-related incidents.
topics: Safe handling and inspection of aircraft wiring systems

onlineTrainings.title: Fuel Tank Safety
onlineTrainings.description: Provides a clear understanding of the risks associated with aircraft fuel tanks and the preventive measures required to ensure compliance with EASA fuel tank safety requirements. Equips staff with the knowledge to identify hazards, prevent ignition sources, and apply best practices when working on or around fuel systems.
topics: Introduction and historical background of fuel tank safety
Hazard awareness during fuel system maintenance

onlineTrainings.title: EASA Part-M & Part-CAMO Essentials
onlineTrainings.audience: Aircraft maintenance and compliance monitoring staff
onlineTrainings.description: Provides a comprehensive overview of continuing airworthiness management under EASA Part-M and Part-CAMO. Designed both for newcomers seeking a structured introduction and for experienced staff wishing to refresh and update their knowledge of the key responsibilities, processes, and regulatory requirements.
topics: EASA Regulatory Framework — role of EASA, Basic Regulation, implementing rules
Part-M Subparts — applicability, responsibilities, continuing airworthiness management
Part-CAMO — scope, privileges, obligations, management system

onlineTrainings.title: Receiving Inspections
onlineTrainings.audience: Part-145, CAMO, or Quality roles staff
onlineTrainings.description: Designed for staff engaged in the handling, inspection, and management of aircraft components within Part-145, CAMO, or Quality roles. Provides the knowledge and skills needed to perform effective receiving inspections and maintain regulatory compliance.

onlineTrainings.title: Train the Trainer
onlineTrainings.subtitle: (for OJT mentors and assessors)
onlineTrainings.audience: Experienced maintenance staff taking on OJT mentor/assessor roles
onlineTrainings.description: Prepares experienced maintenance staff to take on the role of On-the-Job Training (OJT) mentors and assessors within an approved maintenance environment. Participants will develop the skills and confidence needed to guide trainees effectively, perform fair and consistent assessments, and ensure compliance with regulatory and organisational standards.
topics: Aircraft safe release to maintenance and documentation

onlineTrainings.title: Compliance Monitoring & Auditing
onlineTrainings.audience: Compliance monitoring staff and aviation authority inspectors
onlineTrainings.description: Designed for compliance monitoring staff and national aviation authority inspectors involved in quality and compliance oversight. Provides the knowledge and practical skills needed to plan, conduct, and manage effective internal and external audits in accordance with EASA, ICAO, and ISO 19011:2018 standards.
topics: Regulatory requirements (ICAO, EASA, FAA, Transport Canada)
Audit methods, techniques and evidence collection

onlineTrainings.title: Safety Investigation
onlineTrainings.description: Designed for safety, compliance, and maintenance personnel involved in event reporting, occurrence investigation, or safety management functions. Provides the essential knowledge and practical tools to conduct effective internal safety investigations in line with EASA Part-145, Part-CAMO, and ICAO Annex 13 principles.

onlineTrainings.title: Root Cause Analysis
onlineTrainings.description: Designed for compliance, quality, and safety personnel responsible for identifying and addressing the underlying causes of non-conformities, incidents, and audit findings. Provides the knowledge and tools to perform systematic root cause analyses and develop effective corrective actions in accordance with EASA, ICAO, FAA, and IATA guidance.
topics: IOSA corrective action records and documentation

onlineTrainings.title: Safety and Quality Management Processes
onlineTrainings.audience: Management, safety, and compliance monitoring staff
onlineTrainings.description: Designed for management, safety, quality, and compliance monitoring personnel seeking a deeper understanding of how safety and quality management systems interact within an aviation organisation. Provides the knowledge and practical insight needed to develop, implement, and maintain effective SMS and QMS in accordance with EASA Part-145 and Part-CAMO requirements.
topics: SMS and QMS principles and objectives
Occurrence investigation links and reporting systems

_backUpNewsItem.title: Airspace of the Middle East and Persian Gulf
_backUpNewsItem.excerpt: Recent developments in the airspace structure over the Middle East and Persian Gulf region have significant implications for aviation professionals operating in the area.
body: The airspace over the Middle East and Persian Gulf region has undergone notable restructuring in recent months, driven by increased air traffic density and the need for more efficient routing. Aviation authorities across the region — including ICAO Member States and regional bodies — have coordinated updates to sector boundaries, altitude assignments, and procedural requirements.

For maintenance engineers and training organizations, these changes are relevant when preparing documentation for aircraft operating on Middle Eastern routes. Route manuals and operational specifications may require updates to reflect new waypoints and airspace classifications.

EASA-certificated personnel working on aircraft registered with carriers operating in the region should verify that their organization's operational procedures are current. Plane Care Academy continues to monitor regulatory updates to ensure our training materials remain aligned with the latest airspace developments.

We encourage all aviation professionals in our network to consult the latest AIP (Aeronautical Information Publication) supplements for the affected FIRs and to review any NOTAMs issued by the relevant authorities. As always, our team is available to answer questions about how regulatory changes may affect your maintenance licensing and training requirements.

_backUpNewsItem.title: European Plan for Aviation Safety (EPAS) 2026
_backUpNewsItem.excerpt: EASA has published the European Plan for Aviation Safety (EPAS) 2026–2030, outlining strategic safety priorities for the next five years across all aviation domains.
body: The European Union Aviation Safety Agency (EASA) has released the European Plan for Aviation Safety (EPAS) 2026–2030, the strategic framework that guides aviation safety activities across Europe for the coming five-year period. The plan identifies key safety risk areas, sets out actions to mitigate them, and defines responsibilities for stakeholders across the aviation system.

For aircraft maintenance training organizations such as Plane Care Academy, EPAS is an important reference point. The plan reinforces the ongoing commitment to human factors training, continuing airworthiness, and the integration of new technologies into maintenance practices. It also highlights the increasing importance of data-driven safety management and the role of Part-147 organizations in the broader safety ecosystem.

Key areas of focus in EPAS 2026 include: continued development of competency-based training frameworks, enhanced oversight of approved training organizations, and increased attention to emerging risks associated with new aircraft types entering service — particularly those featuring advanced propulsion and digital avionics systems.

Plane Care Academy is committed to aligning its training programs with the safety priorities outlined in EPAS 2026. We will continue to update our curricula and assessment methods to reflect EASA's evolving guidance and to ensure our graduates are equipped to contribute to the highest standards of aviation safety in Europe and beyond.

_backUpNewsItem.title: EASA Updates Part-66 License Categories
_backUpNewsItem.excerpt: EASA has issued updates to Part-66 aircraft maintenance license category requirements, affecting both new applicants and holders seeking category extensions.
body: The European Union Aviation Safety Agency (EASA) has published amendments to Part-66 of the aviation regulations, introducing updates to the requirements for aircraft maintenance license (AML) categories and subcategories. These changes affect both individuals applying for a new licence and existing licence holders seeking to add categories or subcategories.

Among the most significant updates are clarifications to the scope of B1.1, B1.2, B1.3, and B1.4 subcategories, as well as adjustments to the basic knowledge examination requirements for certain modules. The amendments also address the requirements for demonstrating practical experience before a licence can be issued or extended.

For those currently enrolled in or planning to undertake type training, the updated rules reinforce the importance of completing EASA Part-147 approved courses. Type training conducted by approved organizations such as Plane Care Academy continues to be the recognized pathway for meeting the aircraft-type endorsement requirements under Part-66.

Plane Care Academy has reviewed the updated regulatory material and confirms that our type training programs remain fully compliant with the amended Part-66 requirements. Candidates who complete our courses will continue to meet the regulatory standards required for license endorsement. We recommend that all candidates and licence holders read the official EASA amendment documentation and contact our team if they have questions about how these changes affect their specific licensing pathway.
